TryHackme Challenge - Stolen Mount

Analysing network traffic related to an unauthenticated file share access attemp, focusing on potential signs of data exfiltration. To do this I used the document challenge.pcapng stored in the ~/Desktop

I began by using the "cat" command for the document but it was encrypted in traditional chinese and cyberchef was not useful. So I used the command "wireshark -r" and it opened the packets. In Statistics and Protocol Hierarchy Statistics. The READ-PLUS operation appears and allows reading sparse files efficiently. While not inherently malicious, it can be used during data exfiltration. Then using "nfs.opcode ==68" the packet 214 was found with a password:

417263686976652050617373776f72640a0a393065623737323361363537623635393731303061616665663137316439663220286d6435290a

In Cyberchef using From_Hex gave us Archive Password

90eb7723a657b6597100aafef171d9f2 (md5)

Using Free Password Hash Cracker it resulted in: avengers 




Using CyberChef to decode the hex output resulted in a ZIP file. Extracting the ZIP file with the password "avengers" revealed a QR code. Using an online QR scanner, it gave me this: THM{n0t_s3cur3_f1l3_sh4r1ng}

The challenge demonstrated file extraction and decoding of encoded content. 

T1048 – Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1027 – Obfuscated Files or Information
